title: Gate B (P0 REQ-005) absent from sk-code/SKILL.md; orchestrator-only, yet P0 marked complete

findingDetails.evidence: grep for boundary|contract-first|change_class|api/schema|pre-execution in sk-code/SKILL.md returns no gate; the only Gate B logic is orchestrate.md:262 and agent-io-contract.md:130, and agent-io-contract.md:115 forbids leaf agents from carrying the predicate. code.md has no change_class gate either. REQ-005 (spec.md:153) is P0 and names sk-code/SKILL.md as the surface; SC-002 (spec.md:172) and the edge-case table (spec.md:227) assert sk-code-path behavior. Packet reports 15/15 P0, completion_pct:100, Status 'Implemented' (checklist.md:130, impl-summary.md:45); the gap is mapped to CHK-024 marked [P1] (checklist.md:77).
findingDetails.why: A P0 requirement's named acceptance artifact does not exist. Because the contract deliberately keeps Gate B out of leaf agents, the gate never fires on direct sk-code/@code code work (the dominant code path per root CLAUDE.md) and only fires on the narrower @orchestrate dispatch path — so the api/schema/integration coverage the spec promises is materially narrower than implemented. Marking this P0 complete (rather than amended/deferred) overstates closure; the spec's own protocol allows 'user-approved deferral' for P1 only, not P0.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either (a) amend REQ-005/SC-002 to state the predicate is orchestrator/contract-owned and explicitly accept that direct sk-code invocations are not gated, reclassifying the sk-code edit as a tracked deferral; or (b) add the boundary contract-first reminder to sk-code/SKILL.md Phase 0 so the gate covers direct code work. Do not leave a P0 whose named artifact is absent marked 15/15 complete.

title: spec.md internally contradicts itself on where Gate B lives; contradiction never reconciled

findingDetails.evidence: NFR Maintainability (spec.md:209) and REQ-001 require predicates to 'live in one place (@orchestrate) so their scope cannot drift silently across agents', which the shared contract codifies (agent-io-contract.md:115: 'should not spread heuristic copies across leaf agents'). REQ-005 (spec.md:153) simultaneously requires sk-code/SKILL.md to carry the gate. Both are listed as satisfied P0s.
findingDetails.why: The two requirements cannot both be literally true under the chosen centralized design, making REQ-005 unsatisfiable as written. The packet docs resolve it silently in code but never amend the spec, leaving a traceability artifact where a P0 acceptance criterion contradicts the implemented architecture.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile the spec: keep the centralized-predicate design and rewrite REQ-005/SC-002 to reference the orchestrator/contract surface (or an explicit sk-code Phase-0 reminder), removing the literal 'sk-code/SKILL.md requires...' wording that conflicts with REQ-001/NFR.
